description: Pricing, Signup & Self-Service Onboarding - Build the pricing page, Clerk-based signup flow, Stripe billing integration, and 6-step onboarding wizard

userStories.title: Pricing page with plan card
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see VanMan's pricing so that I can evaluate the cost for my business.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /pricing
Single plan card displayed prominently (no confusing tier comparison — single flat plan)
Plan card shows: plan name (e.g. 'VanMan Pro'), monthly price placeholder, 'per month' label
Monthly/Annual toggle switch with annual discount messaging (e.g. 'Save 20% with annual billing')
Price displayed in visitor's regional currency (NZD, AUD, GBP, USD) using Intl.NumberFormat — currency config stored in a pricing config file

Feature inclusion checklist with checkmarks: unlimited jobs, unlimited contacts, unlimited users, all features included, Xero integration, iOS & Android mobile app (drivers, operators, admins), team chat (real-time staff messaging), email & SMS customer communications
Regional currency auto-detected via geo-detection (PW-08 region middleware) or region selector, falling back to NZD
'Start Free Trial' CTA button linking to /signup
'14-day free trial. No credit card required.' text prominently displayed below CTA
Page statically generated (SSG)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Price amounts TBD (open question). Regional currency display connected to PW-08 localization, but basic implementation here.

userStories.title: Pricing FAQ accordion
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want answers to common pricing questions so that I can make a confident decision.
acceptanceCriteria: FAQ accordion section below the pricing card on /pricing page

8 FAQ items: 'What happens after the free trial?', 'Can I cancel anytime?', 'Do I need a credit card to start?', 'Is there a setup fee?', 'What payment methods do you accept?', 'Do you offer discounts for annual billing?', 'Can I change my plan later?', 'What\'s included in the free trial?'
Each item expands/collapses on click using shadcn/ui Accordion component
Only one item open at a time (single accordion mode)
Answers written with clear, friendly, confidence-building tone
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: FAQ answers should directly address objections and build confidence. Tone: friendly, reassuring, transparent about pricing and terms.

userStories.title: Signup page UI
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to create an account so that I can start my free trial.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /signup

Signup form with fields: full name (required), email (required, validated), password (required, with visual strength indicator), company name (required), country/region selector (NZ, AU, UK, US, Other)
'Or sign up with Google' OAuth button
Terms of Service and Privacy Policy checkbox with links to /legal/terms and /legal/privacy
'Already have an account? Log in' link to /login
Form validation with Zod: required fields, valid email format, password minimum 8 characters with strength rules
Submit button shows loading state during form submission
Error handling: displays inline error messages (e.g. 'Email already exists')
On successful signup: redirects to /onboarding
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Links to /legal/terms and /legal/privacy (built in PW-07). Design: minimal fields to reduce friction.

userStories.title: Signup backend — Clerk user and org creation
userStories.description: As a new user, I want my account to be created when I submit the signup form so that I can access VanMan.
acceptanceCriteria: Server action or API route handles signup form submission (server-side, not client-side)
Creates Clerk user with name, email, password using Clerk SDK
Creates Clerk organization (tenant) with company name
Adds user as admin of the new organization
Stores country/region in Clerk user metadata
On success: redirects to /onboarding
On error: returns structured error message to form (e.g. email already exists, password too weak)
Atomic operation: both Clerk user and org creation must succeed or both should roll back on failure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Google OAuth creates same Clerk user/org structure. Atomic guarantee is critical to avoid orphaned users without orgs.

userStories.title: Stripe free trial activation
userStories.description: As a new user, I want my free trial to start automatically so that I can use VanMan immediately without payment.
acceptanceCriteria: After Clerk user/org creation: Stripe customer created via Stripe Node.js SDK server-side
Stripe subscription created in 'trialing' state with 14-day trial period (no payment method required)
Stripe customer ID and subscription ID stored in Clerk organization metadata
Trial end date calculated and stored for display in account settings
No feature restrictions during trial — full access to everything
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stripe calls happen server-side only. Price ID must be configured via environment variable.

userStories.title: Onboarding wizard shell and layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the onboarding wizard multi-step layout so that individual steps can be built on top of it.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /onboarding with multi-step wizard layout
Progress indicator showing 6 steps with labels: Company, Trucks, Team, Xero, Mobile App, Done
Current step highlighted, completed steps show checkmark
Next/Back navigation buttons at bottom of each step
Skip button available on each step (except completion step)
Wizard state persisted (if user closes browser mid-wizard, they resume where they left off) — use URL search params or database
Friendly tone: heading says 'Let\'s get you set up' with encouraging subtext per step
Progress bar clickable to navigate back to previous completed steps
Single-column layout on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Onboarding data writes to the main app's database (Supabase) via API or server actions. Wizard state could be stored in Clerk user metadata or URL params.

userStories.title: Onboarding step 1 — Company setup
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to set up my company details so that VanMan is configured for my business.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 1 form fields: company name (pre-filled from signup), phone number, email, physical address (text input — Google Places autocomplete can be added later), timezone (auto-detected via Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone, editable dropdown), GST/tax number (optional), logo upload (optional, with preview)
Form validated with Zod schema
On Next: saves company data via server action to database
Skip option skips to step 2 without saving
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Company name pre-filled from signup to reduce friction. Timezone auto-detected to save the user from looking it up. Address field is text-only initially; Google Places autocomplete can be added later.

userStories.title: Onboarding step 2 — First truck
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to add my first truck so that I can start scheduling jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 2 form fields: truck name (e.g. 'Van 1', 'Big Truck'), color picker (for calendar color-coding), capacity in cubic meters (optional), registration plate (optional)
'Add another truck' button to add multiple trucks inline (repeating form section)
On Next: saves truck(s) data via server action to database
Skip option skips to step 3 without saving
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Color picker is important because truck colors appear throughout the calendar. Default to a set of distinct, visually appealing colors. Registration plate is optional for businesses that don't track it.

userStories.title: Onboarding step 3 — Invite team
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to invite my team so that my operators and drivers can access VanMan.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 3 form: email input with role selector dropdown (Admin, Operator, Driver)
'Add another' button to invite multiple people (repeating form row)
On Next: sends Clerk invitation emails for each entry via server action
Shows success confirmation for each invitation sent
Skip option skips to step 4 without sending invitations
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Role selector determines what the invited person sees in VanMan. Drivers get a simplified mobile view, Operators get full controls, Admins get everything. Explain roles briefly in the UI.

userStories.title: Onboarding step 4 — Connect Xero
userStories.description: As a new user, I want the option to connect Xero during setup so that invoicing works from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 4 shows 'Connect Xero' button and brief explanation of what connecting Xero enables (one-click invoicing, automatic sync, etc.)
'I\'ll do this later' skip option displayed prominently — this step is clearly optional
Connect Xero button initiates OAuth flow (placeholder link to Xero OAuth — actual integration handled by main app)
If connected: shows success confirmation with Xero org name
If not connected: no error, just moves to next step
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Xero OAuth integration is a complex flow — for the public website this may just link to the main app's Xero connection page.

userStories.title: Onboarding step 5 — Download mobile app
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to download the mobile app during onboarding so that my drivers and field staff can get started.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 5 shows App Store and Google Play download badge placeholders with links
QR code placeholder that would open the app store listing on the user's phone
Brief overview text: 'Your drivers see their assigned jobs, track delivery times, chat with the team, and navigate to addresses — all from their phone'
'Send download link to my team' button placeholder (would send SMS/email with app links to invited team members)
Skip option available
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Mobile app download is promoted here because getting drivers on the app early is critical for adoption. QR code makes it easy to scan from desktop during setup.

userStories.title: Onboarding step 6 — Completion
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to finish onboarding and reach the app so that I can start using VanMan.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 6 shows summary of what was configured: company name, trucks added count, team members invited count, Xero connection status, app download status
'Go to Dashboard' primary CTA button linking to the main app's /dashboard
Quick links: 'Create your first job', 'Explore settings', 'Read getting started guide' (links to /help/getting-started from PW-06)
Confetti or celebratory animation (subtle, using CSS keyframes or a lightweight library like canvas-confetti)
Marks onboarding as complete in Clerk user metadata via server action (won't show wizard again)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: 'Getting started guide' link points to help center (PW-06). 'Go to Dashboard' exits the public website to the main app.

userStories.title: Stripe webhook handler
userStories.description: As the system, I need to handle Stripe webhook events so that subscription state stays in sync.
acceptanceCriteria: API route at /api/webhooks/stripe (POST only)
Webhook signature verification using Stripe SDK constructEvent()
Handles event: customer.subscription.updated — updates subscription status in Clerk org metadata
Handles event: customer.subscription.deleted — updates account state to 'cancelled'
Handles event: invoice.payment_failed — sets 'payment_failed' flag in metadata
On trial expiry without payment method: account moves to 'expired' state with grace period
Returns 200 for handled events, 400 for signature failure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stripe webhook signing secret must be configured via STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET environment variable.

userStories.title: Stripe Customer Portal integration
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to manage my subscription so that I can add a payment method, view invoices, and cancel if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Manage Billing' link accessible from the main app's account settings page
Link opens Stripe Customer Portal (hosted by Stripe) in a new tab or redirect
Customer Portal configured via Stripe dashboard with VanMan branding
Portal allows: update payment method, view invoices, cancel subscription, switch billing period (monthly/annual)
API route /api/billing/portal creates a Stripe portal session and returns the URL
On trial expiry without payment method: account shows 'expired' state with messaging prompting to add a payment method
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The 'Manage Billing' link lives in the main app, but the API route and Stripe portal configuration may be set up in the public website project. Configure portal via Stripe Dashboard.

userStories.title: Login page
userStories.description: As a returning customer, I want to log in so that I can access the main VanMan app.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /login
Clerk SignIn component or custom login form with email/password and Google OAuth button
'Don\'t have an account? Sign up' link to /signup
'Forgot password?' link to Clerk's password reset flow
On successful login: redirects to the main app dashboard
Authenticated users visiting /login are automatically redirected to dashboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This page provides the login entry point on the public website. Actual authentication managed by Clerk.
